query_normalized: can publicly available data reveal subtle violations of time-reversal symmetry in beta decay? physics
rationale: The paper focuses on neutrinoless double beta decay ($0\nu\beta\beta$) and lepton-number violation, which is a distinct physical mechanism from the time-reversal symmetry violations in standard beta decay ($\beta$-decay) queried by the user. While both involve nuclear weak decays, the paper does not measure the specific observable (time-reversal violation) or the specific process (standard beta decay) required by the user's question, failing the "same-mechanism" and "independent/dependent variab
